WARNING: This data is ONLY for CSE and CS branch.
document: Lab Courses Detail Syllabus - 3rd and 4th Semester

course_objectives: Work with PL/SQL and Advanced Database Concepts
practical_list: Creating Entity-Relationship Diagram using case tool (STAR UML) - project-based (Insurance DB, Banking DB, Student DB, Library DB, etc.)
Introduction to data types and database implementation using Create, Insert, and Basic SQL SELECT
Manipulate data in existing tables (UPDATE, DELETE, WHERE conditions)
Create and manage tables with constraints (PRIMARY KEY, FOREIGN KEY, NOT NULL, CHECK) and ALTER structure
Implement JOIN concepts (INNER JOIN, multi-table joins)
Aggregate data using GROUP BY functions and implement sub-queries
Implement concept of views and indexes (CREATE VIEW, CREATE INDEX, DROP VIEW)
Implement concept of PL/SQL (addition, loops, number inverse)
Implement concept of cursor (explicit cursor with salary update logic)
Create procedures, functions and triggers in Oracle (packages, RAISE_APPLICATION_ERROR)

lab_courses.prerequisite: Basic concept of C language and data structure
practical_list: Study of hardware and software requirements of different OS (UNIX, LINUX, WINDOWS, Android, iOS)
Execute various UNIX system calls
Write a basic program on VI Editor in Linux
Shell Scripting
Implement FCFS CPU Scheduling Policy
Implement SJF CPU Scheduling Policy
Implement Priority CPU Scheduling Policy
Implement Round Robin CPU Scheduling Policy
Implementation of Banker's algorithm
Implement Bounded Buffer (producer-consumer) problem using Semaphores
Implement Readers-Writers problem using Semaphore
Implementation First Fit, Best Fit, Worst Fit contiguous allocation technique
Implement file storage allocation technique: Contiguous (array), Linked-list, Indirect allocation (indexing)
Comparison of Disk Scheduling Algorithms

lab_courses.course_name: Object Oriented Programming using Java Lab
lab_courses.prerequisite: Basic concept of C language and data structure
practical_list: Insert 3 numbers and find greatest
Count total odd numbers between 1-100 and display their sum
Find largest and smallest numbers in an array
Count characters in string, reverse string, check palindrome
Sum of command line arguments
Create SimpleObject class with constructor message display
Create Number class with isZero, isPositive, isNegative, isOdd, isEven, isPrime, isAmstrong methods
Create Room class with setData and displayData
Shape class with Circle, Triangle, Square subclasses using polymorphism
Employee class with Manager and Programmer subclasses overriding calculateSalary()
Method overloading, method overriding, and Abstract Class
Employee class with file I/O (read/write to emp.txt)
File operations using exists(), isDirectory(), isFile(), getName(), getAbsolutePath()
Implement Storage, Counter and Printer classes using threads
Create MyThread class extending Thread class
Create MyThread class implementing Runnable interface
Assign different priorities to threads
Producer-consumer using wait() and notify()
Implement deadlock in Java
Lambda Expression, Functional Interface, Method References, Default and Static Methods
Annotations in Java
Filter data using streams
For-each loop with arrays
Base64 Encoding and Decoding
Local Variable Type Inference
Sealed Class
Text Blocks and Records
Linked list operations (reverse order)
HashSet operations
TreeSet operations
Map operations (count key-value, search in TreeMap)
Iterator demonstration
Iterable to Collection conversion
Industry-oriented applications using Spring Framework
RESTful web services testing using Spring Boot

lab_courses.course_name: Design and Analysis of Algorithms Lab
lab_courses.prerequisite: Background in mathematics and strong programming skills
practical_list: Implement and analyze Merge Sort using recurrence relations
Implement Quick Sort and Randomized Quick Sort and compare efficiency
Implement Shell Sort and analyze time complexity
Implement Counting Sort, Radix Sort, Bucket Sort and compare performance
Solve Fractional Knapsack Problem using Greedy approach
Implement Activity Selection Problem using Greedy Strategy
Solve Task Scheduling with Deadline and Penalty using Greedy Strategy
Implement Kruskal's Algorithm for MST
Implement Prim's Algorithm for MST
Implement 0-1 Knapsack Problem using Dynamic Programming
Solve Optimal Binary Search Tree (OBST) problem
Implement Matrix Chain Multiplication using Dynamic Programming
Solve Longest Common Subsequence (LCS) Problem
Implement All-Pairs Shortest Path Algorithm (Floyd-Warshall)
Given coin denominations and target N, determine minimum coins needed and total number of ways
Implement N-Queens Problem using Backtracking
Solve Subset Sum Problem using Backtracking
Implement Graph Coloring using Backtracking
Solve Hamiltonian Cycle Problem using Backtracking
Knight's Tour problem on N×N chessboard
Implement Traveling Salesman Problem (TSP) using Branch and Bound
Implement Naive String Matching Algorithm
Implement Rabin-Karp String Matching Algorithm
Implement Knuth-Morris-Pratt (KMP) String Matching Algorithm
Solve Vertex Cover Problem using Approximation Algorithms
Study of LAN using different Network topologies and network connecting devices (Hub, Switch, Router)
Study and learn handling of networking hardware: NIC card, RJ-45, CAT-6 cable, crimping (T568A and T568B), cable tester
Running and using services/commands: ifconfig, ping, traceroute, nslookup, telnet, ftp
Establish peer to peer network connection using NIC, cables, Switch and Router
Install Wireshark and capture/analyze local traffic
Simulate and Configure a Network topology using Packet Tracer software
Implement data link layer framing: character count, character stuffing, bit stuffing
Implement CRC error correcting code (CRC-12, CRC-16, CRC-CCIPP)
Implement program to determine IP address class (A, B, C, D, E) and translate dotted decimal to 32-bit
Implement Dijkstra algorithm to compute shortest path through a graph
